_note: SYNTHETIC — invented district and facts. ALJ unknown (tests the no-ALJ path). Exercises procedural defects (March-15 mailing, over-noticing on a bumping theory, ADA/FTE prep-period calculation) + PKS reduction of a categorically-funded program.
basis: PKS reduction under Ed. Code 44955: elimination of a categorically-funded (Title I) intervention-coach program whose grant funding lapsed, plus an ADA decline.
reductions: elimination of the categorically-funded Title I intervention-coach positions
procedural_facts: One preliminary notice was mailed March 16 — one day after the deadline — because of an address error in the personnel file; the employee did receive it.
The board resolution adopted Feb 28 specified 4.0 FTE, but the district served preliminary notices on 6 employees on the theory that bumping could reach them.
Respondents contend the district's FTE calculation is wrong because it did not count teacher preparation periods in determining full-time loads.
board_resolution_criteria: Resolution adopted Feb 28 authorizing reduction of 4.0 FTE and elimination of the categorically-funded intervention-coach program.
attorney_concerns: One notice went out March 16 because of a bad address — is that defect fatal? We noticed 6 teachers though the resolution names 4.0 FTE, because of bumping — is over-noticing a problem? And respondents say our FTE math is wrong for not counting prep periods — does that sink the calculation?
